title: Lowkenuinely
about: Lowkenuinely, also spelled as Lowkeuinely, refers to a portmanteau of the words "lowkey" and "genuinely," which was coined in November 2025 as a reaction to the Slang Overload use of the latter. The term mostly emerged from the Chicago Drill Reverse Edits trend as a humorous substitute for the slang "genuinely."
origin: On November 19th, 2025, TikToker[5] @cammmmmm22 shared a video caption meme using the Ashton Hall Throwing His Video Game Controller video and a text overlay caption, reading, "When the pastor at the altar lowkey asks your wife if she wants to be your lawfully wedded wife, and instead of saying 'I do' she genuinely says 'genuinely' and you lowkenuinely tweak out." The video is currently the earliest known use of the term online and it received over 370 likes in a month.
